regimeSub: AI-led equity melt-up meets a hawkish Warsh Fed and Iran-war de-escalation, with CPI stuck at a 3-year high of 4.2%.
themes: Iran ceasefire + tanker flows resuming -> Brent back to $74, energy CPI tailwind fading but base effects keep inflation hot
Warsh Fed drops cutting bias, leaves door open to hike -> front-end repriced, real policy rate still negative at -0.6%
AI capex + power-shortage narrative -> Nasdaq best quarter since 2020, megacap tech masking narrow breadth
Negative real rates + fiscal/geopolitical hedging -> gold pinned above $4,000 even as equities print records

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = negative real rate; Fed behind the curve as it stalls cuts and threatens hikes.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +31bp and modestly steeper; market doubts a hike sticks, prices eventual cuts despite hot CPI.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight; VIX 17 and record equities signal zero credit stress, no recession premium priced.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.2 — complacent after AI-rout snapback; cheap vol given hike risk and frayed Iran ceasefire.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $74, lowest since pre-Iran war as Hormuz tankers exit; supply-surge bet, but stockpile buying risk lurks.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4,056 — refuses to fall; negative real rates and geopolitical hedging keep structural bid intact.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1,551 — won weak despite KOSPI 8,476 melt-up; carry and US rate edge pressure EM Asia FX.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI/semis the engine of the Nasdaq 20% quarter; leadership narrow, power-shortage theme the next leg.

headline: As of 2026-06-30, equities are ripping to records on an AI surge while a new Warsh-led Fed holds at 3.63% with a hike bias against 4.2% inflation, and an Iran ceasefire pulls Brent to $74 — risk-on, but real rates negative and gold above $4,000 flag the unease.
